message:
fix: enforce read/write tracked-buffer bound regardless of repair mode

Tightens the unistd_abi read/write EFAULT guard introduced in the
prior commit. The earlier form only rejected oversized tracked
buffers when repair_enabled was false, on the theory that the
healing path's maybe_clamp_io_len would re-bound the transfer when
repairs are on. In practice the clamp only handles the
clamp-to-known-prefix case; it doesn't cover null-with-positive-len
or pointers whose tracked region cannot fit count bytes at all,
which still need a hard EFAULT before the syscall.

Removes the !repair_enabled gate from both read() and write() so
the tracked_region_fits check fires unconditionally. The clamp logic
following it remains untouched, so legitimate clamp paths still
proceed normally; only the genuinely-broken pointers are rejected.
